claim: PingSkills 'How To Serve Fast And Long' (Alois Rosario, 14 years old, ~1.6M views, 2:50) is the most-watched short tutorial on the long fast serve.
quote: How To Serve Fast And Long | Table Tennis | Pingskills ... PingSkills 258K abonnees ... 1,6 mln. weergaven 14 jaar geleden

claim: Tom Lodziak's 'How to do a SUPER FAST long serve (with Paul Drinkhall)' (May 2019, 5:57, ~211K views) features pro Paul Drinkhall demonstrating the serve as a 3rd-ball-attack setup.
quote: How to do a SUPER FAST long serve (with Paul Drinkhall) ... 211K weergaven 6 jaar geleden ... pro player Paul Drinkhall, shows how to do his super fast long serve ... set up a strong 3rd ball attack

claim: Craig Bryant's '4 Fast & Furious Long Serves | Outsmart Your Opponents' (~1 year old, 88K views, 14:48) is the dedicated long-fast-serve deep-dive on his TT Service Guy channel, covering four variations to push opponents back.
quote: 4 Fast & Furious Long Serves | Outsmart Your Opponents ... 88K weergaven 1 jaar geleden ... Craig Bryant breaks down four variations of long, fast table tennis serves designed to push opponents back ... maximizing speed, using spin as a weapon, and creating deceptive skidding effects

claim: Lodziak x Craig Bryant '4 killer serves to destroy your opponents' (~7 years old, 9.2M views, 11:37) explicitly includes the 'fast down the line' as one of four killer serves and is the most-viewed serve tutorial on Lodziak's channel.
quote: 4 killer serves to destroy your opponents (with Craig Bryant) ... 9,2 mln. weergaven 7 jaar geleden ... The serves in this video include: the kicker, fast down the line, super wide sidespin, and the ghost serve

claim: Lodziak's checklist for an effective long serve is fast, low over the net, bouncing close to opponent's baseline, placed in a corner or to the elbow, and disguised — with the first bounce on your side 'very close to your end of the table'.
quote: be fast ... go low over the net ... bounce close to your opponent's baseline ... be placed in a corner or to a player's elbow ... be disguised ... first bounce on your side must be very close to your end of the table

claim: Lodziak's frequency rule: occasional long serves to surprise active opponents, but up to 80% long serves vs passive players who don't attack — directly justifying the 2-3-per-game ambush usage at club level.
quote: you can use long serves a lot, as much as 80% of the time ... opponents who can attack with enough power that you cannot return the ball ... revert to mostly short/half-long serves

claim: Expert Table Tennis (Ben Larcombe) gives the two non-negotiables for fast serves: contact the ball low (let it drop near net/table height) and aim the first bounce at your own endline corner so the trajectory stays flat and forward.
quote: let the ball drop much further than feels comfortable ... aim for your own endline ... bounce as close to your corner as possible ... hit the ball more forwards instead of down

claim: Learn Table Tennis 'How to Perform a SUPER FAST SERVE?' (Mar 2025, ~181K views, 0:06+) is a recent short-form alternative covering speed, trajectory and spin factors.
quote: How to Perform a SUPER FAST SERVE? - Table Tennis Fast Serve Tutorial ... Learn Table Tennis ... 181K weergaven 1 jaar geleden ... key factors that influence speed, trajectory, and spin

claim: Lodziak's drill collection prescribes specific elbow-targeted serve drills: '3rd Ball Drill 2' has the attacker serve half-long backspin to the feeder's elbow forcing a weak return, and 'Receive Drill 2' practises returning long serves to the backhand or elbow.
quote: 3rd Ball Drill 2: Backspin Serve, Backhand Topspin ... attacker serves half-long backspin to the feeder's elbow ... Receive Drill 2: Backhand or Elbow ... feeder serves long sidespin to either the attacker's backhand or elbow

claim: Craig Bryant runs 'The Table Tennis Service Guy' channel (~80K subs) which is exclusively service-focused, making it the highest-density single source for serve-specific drills including long-fast variations.
quote: Craig Bryant - The Table Tennis Service Guy ... 79,6K abonnees ... A table tennis channel dedicated to improving your service game

claim: Lodziak's site page for the Drinkhall fast long serve confirms the tactical framing: this serve is used to 'catch his opponent out' rather than as a default, supporting the 2-3-per-game ambush thesis.
quote: Paul uses this serve quite often when playing matches to catch his opponent out and set up a strong attacking opportunity on the third ball
